raw_response:
Diplomatic Note to all Powers — Spring 1901 (Movement)

To friends and potential partners:

I write plainly and without flourish. Our fleet posture will be decisive for stability in the northwestern approaches this year. England will act to preserve safe sea lanes, prevent any single continental power from dominating the Channel/North Sea, and protect our home ports. Our current posture:

- Forces: F London, F Edinburgh, A Liverpool.
- Immediate intention: secure the Channel approaches while retaining the flexibility to respond to moves in the North Sea and Atlantic.

Public signal of planned moves (for clarity in negotiation and forecasting)
- F London → English Channel (via SOUTH)
- F Edinburgh → North Sea
- A Liverpool → support coastal security / hold Liverpool (flexible to move to Lpl–Yor or Lpl–Iri if needed)

Interpretation and consequences
- A German advance into the North Sea or a French push to challenge English Channel control will meet our full naval attention. The English Channel will be contested only if necessary; I prefer to hold the Channel as a bargaining chip to shape continental fleets rather than precipitate an immediate clash.
- If Germany concentrates Kie/Ber naval pressure westward, I will prioritize North Sea operations and seek alliance with France (temporary maritime cooperation) or Russia (to keep Germany checked).
- If France demonstrates naval restraint and focuses on land operations, I will avoid direct naval confrontation and aim for tacit agreements guaranteeing Channel neutrality.
- If Russia and Turkey show southern or eastern focus, our likelihood to cooperate with France increases; if either shifts toward the North Sea or Scandinavia, I will treat them as potential threats to sea-lanes and respond accordingly.

Offers and requests (concise)
- To France: I propose a non-aggression understanding at sea for 1901 — I will keep F London in the Channel (or support to Channel) and F Edinburgh in the North Sea so long as France does not blockade or attack English coastal waters. In return I will not move against Brest or Paris and will pass through cooperative information on German naval movements.
- To Germany: Refrain from pushing F Kiel/F Baltic assets aggressively into the North Sea this spring. If you respect maritime boundaries, I will not contest your Baltic interests and will consider limited coordination to keep Russia’s northern fleet contained.
- To Russia: Keep your fleet focused on Black Sea or Baltic aims. Any move northward to threaten Scandinavian or North Sea access will force us into a defensive naval posture.
- To Italy, Austria, Turkey: Your focus on the Mediterranean and Balkans is welcome; I will not interfere there provided those fronts do not produce a continental hegemon that threatens the Channel supply route.

Commitment on timing and leverage
- My naval movements are calibrated to retain rapid redeploy capacity. I will not over-commit fleets early; instead I will use Channel/North Sea presence as leverage to keep alliance options open. If a continental power attempts to seize a dominant position, I will escalate naval cooperation with continental partners to restore balance.

If you wish concrete orders or a written agreement (treaty-style phrasing), propose your specific moves. I value clear, understated commitments and firm timing — name your moves and I will respond with precise support/defense terms.
